data.title: Walmart Stock (WMT) Dials Lower Despite Holding on to PhonePe Stake Post-IPO
data.description:
Shares in retail giant Walmart (WMT) dialed lower today despite reports that it will retain most of its stake in Indian payments firm PhonePe after its upcoming $1.5 billion IPO.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Walmart Keeps in Control

Walmart will cut its stake in PhonePe by about 12% to around 60%. Other stakeholders, Tiger Global and tech titan Microsoft (MSFT), plan to exit their stakes.

Walmart, which holds an about 72% stake in PhonePe, is selling nearly 46 million shares in the IPO. The three firms will together sell around 50.7 million shares in the offering. PhonePe will not issue any new shares in the IPO and will not receive proceeds from the sale. It aims to list by mid-2026, according to a Reuters report.

The company, founded in 2015, is the leading payments platform on India's popular unified payments interface (UPI), commanding more than 45% market share by transaction volume, as of December 2025.

However, PhonePe's loss widened to 14.44 billion rupees ($157.80 million) in the six-month period ended September 30, from 12.03 billion rupees a year ago, while revenue rose about 22% to 39.18 billion rupees, the filing showed.

Its main competitors in India are Google (GOOGL) Pay and Paytm.

Crucial Expansion Plans

PhonePe has more than 657 million registered users and offers payment solutions to over 47 million merchants.

PhonePe had been part of Flipkart, an India-based e-commerce group that is also majority-owned by Walmart. Both have been a crucial part of Walmart's international expansion plans in the world's most populous country.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Applied Digital Stock Slips Even as 430MW AI Data Center Construction Begins
data.description:
Applied Digital (APLD) is continuing to grow its data center business by beginning construction of its newest project, Delta Forge 1. More specifically, this will be a large data center campus capable of supplying 430 megawatts of power and will be located somewhere in the southern United States. While the exact location was not disclosed, CEO Wes Cummins said that the project is an important next step for the company.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Initially, Delta Forge 1 will include two data center buildings, each capable of handling 150 megawatts, spread across a 500-acre site. Importantly, the campus is designed to expand over time as customer needs grow, especially from large cloud and AI users. Applied Digital also expects the facility to be fully operational by mid-2027 and believes it will provide reliable service to customers while delivering long-term benefits to the local community.

Meanwhile, Applied Digital is already making progress on another major project known as Polaris Forge 1 in North Dakota. The site is planned to reach a total capacity of 400 megawatts, and importantly, the first 100 megawatts are already online. Interestingly, like Core Scientific (CORZ), CoreWeave (CRWV), and Hut 8 (HUT), Applied Digital has shifted from its crypto-mining roots to focus on building data centers for AI and high-performance computing.

Is APLD Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on APLD stock based on 11 Buys assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average APLD price target of $49 per share implies 43.4% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Target Stock (TGT) Slides despite Board Additions amid Falling Sales
data.description:
American retailer Target (TGT) on Thursday welcomed two former C-suite executives at Nike (NKE) and Walmart (WMT) to its board. However, TGT shares dropped in late-morning trading as investors failed to cheer the update from the retailer, which is battling declining sales.

The two new additions are John Hoke III, a former chief innovation officer at Nike, and Steve Bartspies, who served as chief merchandising officer at Walmart between 2015 and 2020.

data.title: Moderna Stock (MRNA) Jumps 10% on Positive Results for Cancer Drug
data.description:
Shares of drug maker Moderna (MRNA) are up 10% on Jan. 22 after the company reported positive trial results for its newest cancer drug.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The U.S. pharmaceutical company, whose stock has struggled since the end of the Covid-19 pandemic, announced that five-year data from a Phase 2 clinical trial showed that its new cancer vaccine, in combination with the immunotherapy Keytruda, had reduced the risk of relapse or death for patients with the skin cancer melanoma.

Investors are cheering the results and hoping that Moderna's pipeline of cancer drugs can drive earnings growth at a time when sales of the company's Covid-19 vaccine and core respiratory vaccine business have greatly diminished.

Bright Outlook for Moderna?

Evercore ISI (EVR) wrote in a research note that Moderna's new cancer drug is a "lead oncology asset and a critical value driver beyond respiratory vaccines." Strong sales of its Covid-19 vaccine allowed Moderna to accumulate nearly $20 billion in cash by 2022. However, the company has lost billions of dollars since 2023.

MRNA stock fell nearly 30% in 2025 as investors worried that the company would run out of cash. However, shares are up nearly 70% so far this year as investor sentiment turns positive and excitement builds for Moderna's pipeline of new drugs and medications.

Is MRNA Stock a Buy?

Moderna's stock has a consensus Hold rating among 20 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on one Buy, 15 Hold, and four Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average MRNA price target of $29.93 implies 45.52% downside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: U.S. Consumer Spending Climbs as Shoppers Keep Pounding the Buy Button
data.description:
Sticky inflation and labor market fears have done little to dent the spending appetite of Americans. On Thursday, the Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA) released its personal income and outlays report for both October and November following a delay caused by the 43-day government shutdown.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Consumer spending rose by 0.5% month-over-month in both months, driven by financial services, healthcare, insurance, gas, and other energy goods.

Consumer Spending Jumps amid 'K-Shaped' Economy Worries

Consumer spending accounts for roughly two-thirds of gross domestic product (GDP). Earlier today, the BEA said that third-quarter GDP grew by 4.4%, higher than the consensus estimate of 4.3%.

At the same time, economists remain concerned that spending is concentrated among higher-income households, while lower- and middle-income households pull back, demonstrating a phenomenon coined a "K-shaped" economy. The Fed's Beige Book seemed to confirm this, saying that "spending was stronger among higher-income consumers with increased spending on luxury goods, travel, tourism and experiential activities."

data.title: SOUN Stock Looks Weak on Technical Charts — Buy or Wait?
data.description:

While the company is growing its presence in voice AI and connected cars, sentiment around the stock is mixed. Short-term technical indicators remain weak, suggesting some investors may wait for clearer momentum before buying. At the same time, analysts remain optimistic, pointing to SoundHound's voice AI growth as a potential driver of upside in the months ahead.

SOUN Stock Technical Analysis

data.title: Gold Price Could Rise 10% by Year-End and Hit $5,400 an Ounce, Says Goldman Sachs (GS)
data.description:
Wall Street investment bank Goldman Sachs (GS) raised its year-end gold price forecast by more than 10%, pointing to steady demand from private investors on top of strong buying by central banks and ETFs. The bank lifted its December 2026 gold target to $5,400 an ounce, up from a prior call of $4,900, according to a note dated January 21.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Goldman said the higher forecast assumes that private investors who bought gold as a hedge against policy risks will hold on to those positions through the end of the year.

Why Goldman Is More Bullish on Gold

Goldman said today's gold buying is not tied to one-off events like elections. Instead, investors are using gold to guard against longer-term risks, such as high debt levels and unclear policy direction. Because these risks are unlikely to ease soon, the bank expects investors to hold their gold positions for longer.

Gold prices have already jumped more than 70% over the past year and continue to hit new highs in early 2026. The rise reflects strong demand for safe assets as global tensions grow and concerns increase about the independence of the U.S. Federal Reserve.

Goldman also sees strong support from central banks, which are expected to buy about 60 tons of gold per month in 2026, mainly from emerging markets. In addition, Western gold ETFs have added roughly 500 tons since early 2025. Meanwhile, lower interest rates could add further support, as Goldman expects the Fed to cut rates by another 50 basis points in 2026.

Goldman is not the only firm. Other Wall Street banks also see gold prices rising through 2026 amid ongoing global uncertainty.

Is GS Stock a Buy?

Goldman stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 11 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 5 Buy and six Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average GS price target of $947.60 implies 0.57% downside from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Powell is a 'Disgrace' and 'Must Resign Quickly,' Says Bill Pulte
data.description:
Federal Housing Finance Agency (FHFA) Director Bill Pulte pointed to falling inflation as a key reason why Fed Chair Jerome Powell should step down. Powell's term as Chair is set to end in May 2026, although his term as a Governor extends until January 2028.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"'Too Late' Jerome Powell is a disgrace, and after the below, he must resign very quickly," said Pulte, referencing a Truflation estimate showing 1.21% year-over-year inflation as of January 22.

Pulte Pushes for Powell's Resignation

The latest Consumer Price Index (CPI) update pointed to prices rising by 2.7% in December. The core personal consumption expenditures (PCE) index, which is the Fed's preferred gauge of inflation and excludes volatile food and energy items, increased by 2.8% in November. Both measures are still above the Fed's inflation target of 2%.

Pulte has repeatedly called for Powell's resignation, citing delayed interest rate cuts that have impacted housing affordability.
